benefit: Rs 6,000 per year in three installments of Rs 2,000, paid directly to bank account via DBT
eligibility: Small and marginal farmer families with cultivable landholding, registered on pmkisan.gov.in
required_documents: Aadhaar card, land records, bank account details
real_process: Installments are credited automatically via DBT to the registered, Aadhaar-linked bank account. No SMS link, phone call, or app download is ever required to 'unlock' or 'verify' an installment.

full_name: Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB PM-JAY)
benefit: Health cover up to Rs 5 lakh per family per year for secondary and tertiary hospitalization
eligibility: Families identified via SECC 2011 database or state-specific extension criteria
required_documents: Ayushman card, Aadhaar card, ration card (if applicable)
real_process: Treatment is cashless at empanelled hospitals using the Ayushman card. There is no separate 'activation fee', 'processing charge', or OTP-based unlock step.

benefit: Financial assistance / interest subsidy for construction or purchase of a house
eligibility: Economically weaker sections, low and middle income groups without a pucca house, per income-band criteria
required_documents: Aadhaar card, income certificate, property documents
real_process: Subsidy is credited to the beneficiary's loan account or bank account through the lending bank/DBT. No advance 'processing fee' is collected via UPI or QR code.

benefit: Free LPG gas connection to women from below-poverty-line households
eligibility: Adult woman from a BPL household without an existing LPG connection
location: Available through local LPG distributors nationwide
required_documents: BPL ration card, Aadhaar card, bank account details
real_process: Connection is issued through the local LPG distributor after standard KYC at time of application -- not via a WhatsApp link or remote 'verification call'.

benefit: Short-term credit for agricultural and allied needs at concessional interest (around 4% with timely repayment)
eligibility: Farmers, including tenant farmers and sharecroppers, with land records or equivalent documentation
location: Apply at any public or private sector bank branch
required_documents: Land records, Aadhaar card, passport-size photo
real_process: Applications and renewals are processed only at the issuing bank branch. Banks never ask for KCC renewal via SMS link or demand a 'middleman fee' to process a renewal.

benefit: Crop insurance covering yield loss due to natural calamities, pests, and diseases
eligibility: Farmers growing notified crops in notified areas, loanee and non-loanee farmers both covered
required_documents: Land records, bank account details, sowing certificate
real_process: Claims are assessed via Crop Cutting Experiments / remote sensing and settled to the bank account on record. No separate 'claim release fee' is ever collected from farmers.

benefit: Scholarship amount varies by scheme, disbursed via DBT to student bank account
eligibility: Varies by scheme -- typically income and academic criteria for school/college students
required_documents: Income certificate, institution ID, bank account details, Aadhaar card
real_process: Disbursement happens only after institution verification through the NSP portal. No scholarship scheme asks for an upfront 'processing fee' by UPI to release funds.

benefit: The scheme provides comprehensive support to traditional artisans and craftspeople, including recognition through a PM Vishwakarma certificate and ID card, skill upgradation with basic (5-7 days) and advanced (15+ days) training and a stipend of ₹500 per day, toolkit assistance with an e-voucher of up to ₹15,000, collateral-free credit support with loans up to ₹3 lakh (₹1 lakh first tranche, ₹2 lakh second tranche) at an interest rate of 5% with an 8% government interest subvention, an incentive of ₹1 per digital transaction (up to 100 transactions per month), and marketing support for quality certification, branding, e-commerce linkage, and trade fairs.
eligibility: Applicants must be artisans or craftspeople, at least 18 years of age on the date of registration, engaged in self-employment in the unorganised sector, working with hands and tools in one of the 18 specified family-based traditional trades. They should not have availed loans under similar credit-based schemes of the Central Government or State Government (e.g., PMEGP, PM SVANidhi, MUDRA) in the past 5 years. Only one member from a family (defined as husband, wife, and unmarried children) is eligible. Individuals in government service and their family members are not eligible.
location: Nationwide, covering both rural and urban areas across India.
required_documents: Aadhaar card, Mobile number, Bank account details, Ration card (or Aadhaar card of all family members if a ration card is not available).
contact_info: Toll-free numbers: 18002677777, 17923. Champions Desk (Ministry of MSME) Email: champions@gov.in, Contact Number: 011-23061574.
real_process: Eligible beneficiaries must register through Common Service Centres (CSCs) by completing mobile verification and Aadhaar e-KYC on the PM Vishwakarma portal. The application undergoes a three-step verification process, including verification at the Gram Panchayat/Urban Local Body (ULB) level, vetting and recommendation by the District Implementation Committee, and approval by the Screening Committee. After successful registration, artisans receive a PM Vishwakarma Digital ID and Certificate. They can then avail benefits such as skill training (basic training is mandatory for the first loan tranche), toolkit incentives, and apply for collateral-free 'Enterprise Development Loans'. The first loan tranche (up to ₹1 lakh) is disbursed upon completion of basic training, while the second tranche (up to ₹2 lakh) is available after repaying the first loan, maintaining a standard loan account, adopting digital transactions in their business, and/or completing advanced training.
